what_it_does: Implements the gallery drawer for the generative grammar engine: loads grammar.json, exposes resolver functions matching the Node engine, builds interactive controls for base unit, signature ratio, density, tier, and proportion, and renders a live 16:9 preview plus a numerical readout from the same derived values. It also persists grammar edits and applies density-driven spacing across the gallery document and piece iframes.
observations: GRAM holds the fetched grammar.json in memory; only base.unit and signature_ratio.value are mutated as grammar edits, while density/tier/proportion are UI selections in gmSel.
renderGrammar() is the single draw routine: it recomputes all derived spatial and typographic values and rebuilds both the preview DOM and the readout from the same numbers.
The resolver functions gmUnit, gmSpace, gmRadius, gmDisplayType, and gmZones are ported to match engine/prove/resolve.mjs so the browser emits the same numbers as the Node script.
applySpace() and applySpaceFrame() inject density-scaled --dna-space-* CSS variables into the gallery document and every shown/thumbnail piece iframe.
The preview uses hardcoded neutral specimen content (Quarry, Atelier, Almanac) so the grammar language is visible without carrying real deck content.
Warmth colors are pulled from the Tokens drawer via ctx.tok()/ctx.surfaces()/ctx.warmth(), with a fallback that reads :root custom properties.
The module registers itself with DNA.gallery and publishes ctx.gram(), ctx.gm helpers, ctx.loadGrammar, ctx.applySpace, and ctx.applySpaceFrame.
standouts: The explicit 'one function draws' contract: every control mutates state and calls renderGrammar(), eliminating duplicated derivation logic.
Density is treated as a spatial twin of warmth: a global dial that re-spaces the whole instrument live via CSS variable injection.
Browser/Node numerical parity is a first-class goal, with the resolver ported verbatim from resolve.mjs.
conventions: IIFE gallery-module registration under global.DNA.gallery with an init(ctx) callback.
gm-prefixed helper functions and gmSel state object.
Inline style-driven DOM construction using template literals.
Async API wrappers around DNA.api.grammar and DNA.api.saveGrammar.
Publishing shared capabilities onto ctx for cross-drawer coordination.
concerns: Large inline HTML/CSS strings in renderGrammar() make the layout hard to maintain and blur the boundary between structure and style.
Heavy reliance on external globals and ctx methods (DNA.api, DNA.injectSpace, ctx.tok, ctx.surfaces, ctx.warmth) with limited defensive checks.
Direct innerHTML assignment on every slider input may be acceptable but is a potential injection surface if any interpolated value becomes user-controlled.
Cross-origin piece iframes will silently fail to receive injected spacing despite the try/catch.
notes: This file is the interactive grammar workbench: it turns grammar.json into sliders, a live canvas, and a transparent arithmetic readout, while exporting density spacing to the rest of the gallery.
questions: What is the exact grammar.json schema that GRAM expects?
How does DNA.injectSpace map density to --dna-space-* variables?
What contract do ctx.tok(), ctx.surfaces(), and ctx.warmth() provide, and what happens if the Tokens drawer is absent?
Where are the --gm-* color variables defined, and are they guaranteed to exist?
Is the grammar preview intended to be the canonical editor, or is it a read-only inspection surface with only two mutable fields?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it is described as the generative grammar engine drawer and it does load the grammar, expose resolvers, provide controls, and render a live preview/readout.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for derived values.
Numerical parity between design-time and runtime.
Spatial systems as a tunable design material.
Cross-drawer composition via a shared ctx.
Neutral specimen content to keep the focus on the system, not the payload.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make an abstract generative grammar feel concrete and trustworthy by giving designers immediate visual and arithmetic feedback, while ensuring that any number shown in the browser could also be produced by the Node resolver.
novelty: The density dial as a global spatial twin to the warmth dial, with per-iframe CSS-variable injection so migrated pieces inherit the chosen spacing.
contribution: A live, numerically faithful grammar preview surface that derives spacing, type, radius, and zone bands from grammar.json; it exposes resolver helpers and density-spacing hooks that other gallery drawers can reuse.
summary_for_embedding: Interactive gallery drawer that loads a generative grammar.json, exposes a browser resolver matching the Node engine, and renders a live 16:9 preview plus arithmetic readout from base unit, signature ratio, density, tier, and proportion selections.
intention_for_embedding: To give designers a hands-on, numerically faithful surface for tuning and inspecting the grammar-derived spacing, type, and radius scales that drive the instrument.

symbols.does: Selects a DOM element by id.
symbols.description: Local shorthand for document.getElementById, used throughout the drawer to grab controls and the preview stage.

symbols.does: Fetches grammar.json and initializes the drawer.
symbols.description: Calls DNA.api.grammar() to populate GRAM, hydrates the control panel, triggers the first render, and applies the standard density spacing.

symbols.does: Injects density-scaled spacing variables into the gallery and its iframes.
symbols.description: Uses DNA.injectSpace to set --dna-space-* values on the host document and on every visible frame/thumbnail iframe so the whole instrument re-spaces with the density dial.

symbols.does: Computes the scaled base unit for a tier.
symbols.description: Multiplies GRAM.base.unit by the selected scale_tiers tier factor; used as the foundation for space, radius, and type derivations.

symbols.does: Resolves a named spatial step for tier and density.
symbols.description: Returns Math.round(unit(tier) * steps[name] * densityMultiplier), matching the Node resolver.

symbols.does: Resolves a corner radius by exponentiating the signature ratio.
symbols.description: Returns Math.round(unit(tier) * ratio^n), producing the modular radius scale.

symbols.does: Resolves a display type size by ratio exponent and tier.
symbols.description: Returns Math.round(pivot * tierFactor * ratio^n), producing the headline/key-figure type scale.

symbols.does: Maps a proportion archetype to pixel bands along an axis.
symbols.description: Multiplies each part in the archetype by the given axis length and rounds, yielding title/main/foot band heights.

symbols.does: Lists archetypes that have a parts array.
symbols.description: Filters GRAM.proportion.archetypes so the proportion dropdown only shows eligible multi-part layouts.

symbols.does: Retrieves the current warmth color surfaces.
symbols.description: Prefers the Tokens drawer's engine via ctx.tok()/ctx.surfaces()/ctx.warmth(), falling back to reading :root custom properties so the grammar preview matches the live palette.

symbols.does: Builds and wires the grammar control UI.
symbols.description: Populates the base-unit slider, signature-ratio slider, density segmented buttons, tier dropdown, and proportion dropdown, attaching oninput/onchange handlers that mutate state and call renderGrammar().

symbols.does: Scales the 1280x720 preview box to fit its responsive stage.
symbols.description: Uses a CSS transform based on stage clientWidth divided by GM_W, recalculated on resize when the drawer is open.

symbols.does: Recomputes and redraws the preview and readout.
symbols.description: Derives unit, margins, gaps, padding, radii, type sizes, and zone bands from the current GRAM and gmSel selections, then builds the preview DOM and a line-by-line arithmetic readout from the same values.

symbols.does: Pads a string to a fixed width.
symbols.description: Small helper used only inside the readout builder to align numeric expressions.

symbols.does: Persists the in-memory grammar back to grammar.json.
symbols.description: Calls DNA.api.saveGrammar(GRAM) and toasts success or failure; only base.unit and signature_ratio.value are expected to have changed.

symbols.does: Downloads the current GRAM as grammar.json.
symbols.description: Creates a Blob from JSON.stringify(GRAM) and triggers a browser download named grammar.json.

symbols.does: Reloads the saved grammar and resets controls.
symbols.description: Calls loadGrammar() again to discard in-memory edits and restore the persisted state.

symbols.does: Opens or closes the Grammar drawer.
symbols.description: Toggles the gmdrawer open class, updates the button state, closes the Tokens drawer if open to avoid stacked overlays, and renders the preview.
